title: Odisha Braces for Heatwave from April 18 to 20 - Pragativadi
full_text:
Odisha continues to reel under intense heat as the Regional Meteorological Centre, Bhubaneswar, issued a warning for hot and humid conditions across 13 coastal districts for the next two days.
The alert covers districts including Balasore, Bhadrak, Kendrapara, Jagatsinghpur, Cuttack, Jajpur, Dhenkanal, Angul, Khordha, Puri, Nayagarh, and Ganjam. Meteorologists advised residents to avoid prolonged exposure to the sun and stay hydrated.
Officials said temperatures have soared above normal in several parts of coastal Odisha, with humidity levels adding to discomfort. The department also forecast isolated thunderstorms with lightning in some areas, urging caution for outdoor workers and farmers.
The heatwave warning extends from April 18 to April 20 for coastal, western, and southern Odisha. District administrations have been asked to prepare cooling shelters and ensure water availability in public areas.
Health authorities advised people to wear light clothing, consume plenty of fluids, and avoid strenuous activities during peak afternoon hours. Schools and workplaces may adjust schedules to minimise exposure.
Meteorologists noted that the combination of high temperature and humidity could lead to heat stress, especially among vulnerable groups such as children and the elderly.
The Regional Meteorological Centre continues to monitor conditions closely and will update forecasts as needed.

title: Rain To Lash Cuttack & 3 Other Odisha Dists In 3 Hrs; Temp To Soar With Return Of Heatwave - OdishaBytes
full_text:
Bhubaneswar: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a nowcast warning for four districts in Odisha till around 6 pm on Wednesday.
Moderate thunderstorms, rain, lightning and hail with wind speed 40-50 kmph may occur at a few places in Angul, Deogarh, Cuttack, Nayagarh during the next 3 hours.
The midday bulletin hs also warned of thunderstorm accompanied by lightning and gusty surface wind (30-40 kmph) in the afternoon/evening hours at one or two places in Mayurbhanj, Balasore, Keonjhar, Bhadrak, Kendrapada, Jajpur, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri and Khurda districts.
Hot and humid conditions may also prevail at isolated places in Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapada, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati.
Similar thunderstorms and sultry weather warnings continue for the state till April 21. Heatwave may also return from April 17. Warning has been issued for Balangir, Jharsuguda, Sambalpur and Sonepur, Angul, Bargarh, Deogarh, Boudh & Kandhamal till April 19.
The MeT office has also forecast a gradual rise in the day temperature) by 2-4°C during the next 2 days. There will be no large change during the subsequent 2 days following which mercury may rise further by 2-3°C across the state, it added.
